gpt55_review_summary: Initial review found nested .dist-info paths could pass and build tags were too permissive. I changed filename parsing to require numeric-leading build tags, required a single root-level .dist-info directory matching filename distribution/version, added tests for the invalid shapes, reran full tests/build and a real-wheel smoke, and re-review returned clean.
verification: poetry run pytest -q tests/test_release_bundle.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py
GPT-5.5 review clean after resolving nested .dist-info and build-tag findings
what_changed: Tightened release-bundle package wheel provenance validation.
Required valid wheel filename grammar, root-level filename-matching .dist-info metadata, Name: document-kv-cache, and non-empty Version.
Added regression coverage for invalid build tags, nested .dist-info, mismatched .dist-info versions, wrong package names, and missing metadata versions.
why: Release bundles should prove the tested wheel is the document-kv-cache package under review, not just any wheel-shaped zip file.
